title: illipe may
intro: Illipe may, also known as Gmelina arborea, is a tree species native to the tropical forests of Asia. It is a deciduous tree with medicinal properties, including the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of illipe may include the potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antidiabetic and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of illipe may can be made into teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various ailments.
